pr-evidence/vllm-runtime-contract-diagno.gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 reviewer Carver found a missing cachet.vllm_runtime_contract_data facade and internally inconsistent installed-vLLM diagnostic records being accepted. The PR added the facade, derived public-submodule import coverage, package_version type checking, recomputed missing/extra/ok validation, and regression tests; scoped re-review reported both findings resolved with no blockers.

GPT-5.5 reviewer found Cachet facade and validator consistency gaps; both were fixed and scoped re-review reported no blockers
what_changed: Single-sourced the vLLM V1 KV connector lifecycle contract data under document_kv_cache and reused it from native-probe diagnostics and the vendored vLLM adapter package.
Added an installed-vLLM KVConnectorBase/SupportsHMA drift diagnostic that reports import errors, missing required methods, extra installed hooks, allowed properties, and fail-closed ok status.
Added the Cachet-branded public module facade and regression tests for strict installed-runtime diagnostic validation.
pr-evidence/vllm-runtime-contract-diagno.why: The g6/L4 Databricks native-probe path needs a local preflight that can detect vLLM runtime API drift before launching provider-backed engine probes, while preserving strict native-probe validation against no-op or test-double wiring.
